clauses.statement: Guarded Run delegates through an injected provider-neutral coordinator whose adapters expose only probe, start, status, cancel, and collect_result, then persists returned state authority-first.

test_refs.case: ARA-S-1 ARA-S-2 provider-neutral contract probes capability before start
ARA-S-1 ARA-S-3 ARA-S-4 Guarded Run persists adapter state and bridges completed review provenance into Agent Review

clauses.statement: Capability mismatch, unavailable runtime, quota, permission wait, timeout, cancellation failure, and malformed results fail closed and never become implementation completion or Gate pass; recovery to running clears stale Run-level stop state, while uncertain start failures require terminal containment acknowledgement or become orphaned.

test_refs.case: ARA-S-6 timeout and malformed success never become completion
ARA-S-2 permission wait clears stale Run stop after provider resumes
ARA-S-5 generic start failure is contained before redispatch can occur

clauses.statement: Implementation completion permits the managed worktree HEAD to advance, but rebinds Run authority only after the reported result HEAD matches the actual managed worktree HEAD; review dispatches require a provider-confirmed read-only sandbox plus started identity and session or thread, results must correlate to that started runtime, and the Agent Review bridge revalidates deterministic dispatch identity, persisted read-only sandbox and review-only capabilities, current HEAD, no changed files, requested/start/result identity, start/result session correlation, implementation-session separation, parallel provenance, and closed lifecycle before recording.

test_refs.case: ARA-S-4 review requires separate identity and closed parallel provenance
ARA-S-4 review result must correlate to the reviewer runtime returned by start
ARA-S-4 review start without session or thread provenance is contained
ARA-S-4 review dispatch identity changes with the implementation session
ARA-S-4 review runtime is read-only at request and result boundaries
ARA-S-4 review rejects a mutable provider sandbox before start
ARA-S-4 review validates started identity and contains the wrong runtime
ARA-S-1 ARA-S-3 ARA-S-4 Guarded Run persists adapter state and bridges completed review provenance into Agent Review
ARA-S-3 Guarded Run collects an implementation result after the managed worktree HEAD advances and rebinds authority
ARA-S-3 Guarded Run rejects an implementation result whose reported HEAD differs from the managed worktree
ARA-S-4 Agent Review bridge revalidates persisted review provenance fail closed

clauses.statement: A fake adapter proves success, quota or capability wait, terminally acknowledged start/status/result timeout containment, generic start failure containment, cancellation, orphan detection with restart suppression, duplicate suppression, and separate read-only reviewer behavior.

test_refs.case: ARA-S-5 duplicate dispatch is reused and cancel confirms terminal runtime
ARA-S-5 start timeout invokes dispatch-scoped force containment
ARA-S-5 start timeout is orphaned unless dispatch-scoped containment confirms terminal status
ARA-S-5 generic start failure is contained before redispatch can occur
ARA-S-5 operation timeout reports an orphan when containment cannot confirm terminal state
ARA-S-5 result timeout contains the provider runtime before failing

diagrams.rationale: Shows the provider-neutral capability probe, dispatch lifecycle, fail-closed stop paths, and structured result handoff back to Guarded Run authority.
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Run[Guarded Run authority] --> Coordinator[Runtime coordinator]
  Coordinator --> Probe[Adapter capability probe]
  Probe -->|available and capable| Start[Provider-neutral start]
  Probe -->|unavailable quota permission or missing capability| Wait[Typed waiting_for_runtime]
  Start --> Runtime[Provider agent runtime]
  Runtime --> Status[status and cancel]
  Status -->|completed| Result[collect structured result]
  Status -->|timeout failure malformed or orphaned| Fail[Typed failed stop]
  Result --> Run
  Wait --> Run
  Fail --> Run

diagrams.rationale: Shows fail-closed boundaries for stale or impersonated review evidence, ambiguous provider timeouts, duplicate dispatch, and orphan containment.
diagrams.mermaid:
flowchart LR
  Input[HEAD and implementation session] --> Coordinator[Runtime coordinator]
  Coordinator -->|deterministic dispatch id| Adapter[Provider adapter]
  Adapter --> Runtime[Agent runtime]
  Runtime -->|timeout or uncertain state| Contain[normal then force containment]
  Contain -->|terminal confirmed| Failed[typed failed stop]
  Contain -->|not confirmed| Orphan[orphaned_agent block]
  Runtime --> Review[review result]
  Review -->|HEAD identity session lifecycle valid| Gate[Agent Review recording boundary]
  Review -->|stale impersonated same session or open| Failed
